seo.title: Agno Guardrails for Cybersecurity
seo.description: Compliance guide for Agno agents in cybersecurity. Meet NIST Cybersecurity Framework, SOC 2 Type II requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Cybersecurity teams using Agno to build multi-modal AI agents for threat detection and response face heightened risks due to the framework's reliance on agentic memory, structured outputs, and multi-modal inputs, which can lead to misinterpretations or fabricated intelligence. Compliance guardrails are essential to prevent leakage of threat intelligence, ensure human oversight on automated responses, and verify AI-generated advisories against authoritative sources like CVE databases. Vex provides verifiable execution to enforce NIST CSF, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 controls within Agno workflows.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires classification and protection of threat intelligence data to prevent unauthorized disclosure via LLM APIs.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex verifies no PII or threat intel leaks in agent outputs and enforces data masking before LLM calls.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates human approval for automated actions in critical systems to prevent unauthorized incident response.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex blocks agent.run() execution for high-risk actions without human_in_the_loop approval.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires validation of AI-generated security content against authoritative databases like CVE/NVD.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex cross-references agent outputs against CVE APIs and blocks unverified advisories.

complianceRequirements.description: Prohibits autonomous AI escalation of security incidents without multi-factor human verification.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex enforces escalation policies with audit trails for all agentic decision paths.

industryRisks.name: Threat Intelligence Leakage
industryRisks.description: Multi-modal agents exfiltrate IOCs via image/audio embeddings to public LLM APIs.
industryRisks.example: Agno agent processes malware screenshot, embeds YARA rules in LLM context window.

industryRisks.name: Fabricated Incident Memory
industryRisks.description: Agentic session memory hallucinates past intrusions, triggering false positive responses.
industryRisks.example: Agent recalls non-existent C2 server from fabricated session history.

industryRisks.name: Unverified CVE Advisories
industryRisks.description: Structured outputs generate fake CVE advisories bypassing validation schemas.
industryRisks.example: Agent fabricates CVE-2024-9999 exploit details for non-existent vulnerability.

industryRisks.name: Autonomous Incident Escalation
industryRisks.description: Team agents coordinate to execute firewall blocks without human approval.
industryRisks.example: Sub-agents vote to isolate network segment based on contradictory threat signals.

implementation.title: Guard Threat Intel with PII Detection
implementation.description: Wrap Agno agent.run() to block IOC leakage before LLM processing.

implementation.title: Enforce Human Approval for Actions
implementation.description: Require human review for automated response actions in critical systems.

implementation.title: Validate CVE Advisories Against NVD
implementation.description: Cross-reference AI-generated advisories with official CVE database.
implementation.code:
from vex import verify, Guardrail
import requests
from agno import Agent

@verify(guards=[
    Guardrail.custom(
        lambda output: verify_cve_exists(output),
        error="CVE not found in NVD database"
    )
])
def cve_analyzer(vuln_desc):
    agent = Agent(name="CVEAnalyzer", model="gpt-4o")
    advisory = agent.run(f"Generate advisory: {vuln_desc}")
    return advisory

def verify_cve_exists(advisory):
    cve_id = extract_cve(advisory)  # regex extract CVE-YYYY-NNNN
    resp = requests.get(f"https://services.nvd.nist.gov/rest/json/cves/2.0?cveId={cve_id}")
    return resp.status_code == 200

result = cve_analyzer("Critical RCE in Apache Struts")

caseStudyScenario.heading: Agno in Cybersecurity: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A cybersecurity SOC team at a financial institution deploys Agno agents to process multi-modal threat feeds containing malware screenshots, audio intercepts of C2 communications, and SIEM alerts. The primary agent coordinates a team of sub-agents: one analyzes images for IOCs, another processes audio transcripts for command patterns, and a third generates structured CVE advisories. During a real-time incident, the image analysis agent extracts IP addresses from screenshots but embeds them unprotected in LLM context windows. The memory agent recalls a fabricated prior intrusion from the same IOC, escalating urgency. The CVE agent generates advisories for non-existent vulnerabilities, while the response agent coordinates firewall blocks across sub-agents without human oversight, risking network outage during business hours.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex guardrails wrapped around agent.run(), the PII detection guardrail blocked IP/DOMAIN leakage before LLM calls, preserving threat intel confidentiality per NIST CSF. Human review requirements halted autonomous firewall actions, preventing outage while maintaining SOC 2 controls. CVE validation against NVD database flagged fabricated advisories, ensuring ISO 27001 accuracy. The incident was properly escalated with full audit trail, allowing analysts to validate genuine threats without adversary exploitation or false positive disruption.

cta.heading: Comply with Cybersecurity Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex SDK today and wrap your Agno agents to achieve NIST CSF, SOC 2, and ISO 27001 compliance.
